name: Falsy Default Trap
description: Logical OR (||) used as a default where the left operand derives from a parameter. Valid falsy values (0, empty string, false) are silently replaced by the default.
smt2Template:
(declare-const {{param}}_value Int)
(declare-const result Int)
(declare-const default_value Int)
(assert (= {{param}}_value 0))
(assert (= result default_value))
(assert (not (= result {{param}}_value)))
(check-sat)

teachingExample.smt2:
(declare-const port Int)
(declare-const result Int)
(assert (= port 0))
(assert (= result 3000))
(assert (not (= result port)))
(check-sat)

smt2ProofTemplate:
(declare-const {{param}}_value Int)
(declare-const result Int)
; Guard: ?? used instead of || (nullish coalescing preserves falsy values)
(assert (= result {{param}}_value))
; Can result differ from input?
(assert (not (= result {{param}}_value)))
(check-sat)
; unsat → proven: falsy values preserved
